title: The Angels have returned
wikitext:
[]: "The Angels have returned"             Quote Post by Replicator » Sat Nov 24, 2012 9:15 pm I recently rewatched 1.11, and noticed when Misato and Ritsuko and discussing EVA Unit-00's reactivation, Ritsuko says (approximately) "The Angels have returned...". Is it possible in Rebuild, the Angels attacked in two waves (we know virtually nothing about the development of the Evangelions so far in Rebuild)? The Third Angel seen in 2.22 might be an example, kept in suspended animation, and studied at Bethany Base. Or is this just another reference to the Adam(s)? But it isn't like the Second Impact was directly linked Angels, in the sense of Adam's Children. Basically we don't know enough about Rebuild's Angel/Evangelion mechanics yet (especially after the release of 3.0) to make conclusive judgements. Discuss...

[]:             Quote Post by BlueBasilisk » Sat Nov 24, 2012 10:21 pm Don't they also say this line in the series? I know Gendo says "The Angels are back" during episode 1 for sure.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Nov 24, 2012 10:29 pm It's just a generic reference in the same vein as NGE. "Second Impact had something to do with Angels. Now there are more Angels, and they're attacking us here in Tokyo-3!" Blue Basilisk wrote: I know Gendo says "The Angels are back" during episode 1 for sure.

Article: The Angels have returned
[]: "The Angels have returned"             Quote Post by Replicator » Sat Nov 24, 2012 9:15 pm I recently rewatched 1.11, and noticed when Misato and Ritsuko and discussing EVA Unit-00's reactivation, Ritsuko says (approximately) "The Angels have returned...". Is it possible in Rebuild, the Angels attacked in two waves (we know virtually nothing about the development of the Evangelions so far in Rebuild)? The Third Angel seen in 2.22 might be an example, kept in suspended animation, and studied at Bethany Base. Or is this just another reference to the Adam(s)? But it isn't like the Second Impact was directly linked Angels, in the sense of Adam's Children. Basically we don't know enough about Rebuild's Angel/Evangelion mechanics yet (especially after the release of 3.0) to make conclusive judgements. Discuss...
